justification: A is strictly accurate: it includes only the four core skills clearly demonstrated (JavaScript, HTML, CSS, Git) and excludes everything problematic. B introduces issues: GraphQL is borderline — the candidate explicitly noted they only used read-only queries written by senior devs, making it a weak claim; localStorage is a browser API feature, not a standalone skill; and VS Code/Chrome DevTools are generic tools of marginal value. More critically, B risks inflating the profile with skills that are either not truly the candidate's own or are trivial. A's conservatism is appropriate given the thin, junior profile. Neither output extracts projects or impact highlights, so both score neutral on those dimensions. A wins on skill_accuracy and is better calibrated overall.
